label: 137_impact bonds_impact bond_dib_sib
description: Impact bonds combine results-based financing with impact investing, providing innovative funding for social programs. They have gained traction globally, especially in addressing post-COVID-19 challenges, by aligning financial returns with positive social outcomes.

earliest_articles.title: Advancing Social Impact Measurement to Build an Asset Class: The Appeal of Social Impact Bonds
earliest_articles.excerpt: Social Impact Bonds represent a new investment vehicle that can advance social impact measurement and attract new sources of funding for critical community development services. newest_articles.excerpt: After several years of mostly virtual convenings and events, 2023 brought us back to in-person events around the world and allowed us to learn and share with each other. newest_articles.title: A review of outcomes-based financing in 2022 and beyond
newest_articles.excerpt: While 2022 saw a return to pre-pandemic normalcy in many regards, the economic and social upheavals around the world due to the pandemic continue to reverberate. central_articles.title: Paying for social outcomes: A review of the global impact bond market in 2017
central_articles.excerpt: 2017 was an exciting year for social and development impact bonds around the globe, with 32 new contracts signed. This brings the total up to 108 contracted impact bonds globally, along with many more in design. This year also marked the contracting of the first social impact bond (SIB) in a low- or middle-income…

central_articles.title: Harnessing private capital and tying funding to results to build back better
central_articles.excerpt: When the world begins to emerge from the COVID-19 crisis, it will be saddled with social and economic challenges of an immense scale. In this policy brief, we recommend the use of outcome-based or pay for success financing—including social and development impact bonds (SIBs and DIBs)—to spur renewal and prosperity by…

central_articles.title: The future of impact bonds globally: Reflections from a recent Brookings event
central_articles.excerpt: “For a not-for-profit it’s the equivalent of venture capital,” said Sir Ronald Cohen, chairman of the Global Social Impact Investing Steering Group, about impact bonds in his keynote address at a recent event at the Brookings Institution.
